description: Letter from the ḥaver ʿEli b. ʿAmram to the physician Avraham b. Furāt ha-Kohen. In Hebrew and Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: 1051–62 CE, as Daniel b. ʿAzarya is invoked as gaʾon. ʿEli opens with a long rhymed poem in honor of the addressee, then goes on to complain about his loneliness and apologize for not being able to come to the addressee in person, as he is unable to leave his office (dukkān) unattended and nobody can take his place. Mann originally published this letter and interpreted dukkān as "shop"; Goitein pointed out that it can also mean the office of a communal leader.
description_he: מכתב בHebrew, Judaeo-Arabic מהמאה ה-11. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
